data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="helix-ada6e67e">
    <title>Complete HELIX's artifact-type metadata across all artifact directories</title>
    <description>
HELIX's methodology defines a catalog of artifact types (Vision, PRD, FEAT, US, ADR, SD, TD, TP, etc.). Each artifact type ships type-level metadata (id, prefix, phase, output location and naming, prompt and template pointers, examples, id_format) that lets any substrate (the DDx tracker today, potentially others) register and reason about that artifact type.

Today the metadata is incomplete and inconsistent:
- Five artifact directories under workflows/phases/ have no meta.yml at all (product-vision, business-case, competitive-analysis, opportunity-canvas, implementation-plan) — they are invisible to any registry that walks meta.yml files.
- Fourteen dependencies.yaml files across artifact directories are home-rolled and parallel to meta.yml — pure cruft. The instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists; type-level dependency declarations are not consumed by any substrate today.
- Existing meta.yml files carry a dependencies: block that no consumer reads.

Scope:
1. Create meta.yml for every artifact missing one. Fields: artifact.{id, prefix, phase}, output.{location, naming}, id_format.{prefix, pattern, example}, prompts.{generation, review}, template.file, examples[].
2. Delete all dependencies.yaml files under workflows/phases/.
3. Strip the dependencies: block from every existing meta.yml.

HELIX defines this metadata as an open schema. DDx happens to consume a subset of it (see DDx's cli/internal/artifacttypes/loader.go), and the field set chosen here should be a strict superset of what any single substrate parses — so any compliant consumer works out of the box. No graph data is lost — the instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
Every workflows/phases/*/artifacts/*/ directory contains meta.yml, template.md, and prompt.md. rg dependencies.yaml workflows/ returns no matches. No existing meta.yml carries a dependencies: block. The DDx artifact-types loader (one consumer) sees every HELIX artifact type; a substrate-neutral walk of the meta.yml inventory enumerates the same set.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Folded into runtime-neutral collapse critical path: complete the artifact-type metadata before schema/docs/skill portability work. DDx is one consumer, not the schema authority.
    </notes>
    <labels>helix, phase:build, kind:implementation, area:artifacts, area:workflow</labels>
    <metadata parent="helix-f9207605" spec-id="FEAT-008" base-rev="b561d59bae972db21d93492f54466b10a17635f0" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-008" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-artifact-template-quality.md">Feature Specification: FEAT-008 — Artifact Template Quality and Completeness</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All four acceptance criteria for helix-ada6e67e already pass at base-rev b561d59 without source changes:

- **AC1**: every `workflows/phases/*/artifacts/*/` dir already has `meta.yml`, `template.md`, `prompt.md` (the five dirs the bead description names as missing — product-vision, business-case, competitive-analysis, opportunity-canvas, implementation-plan — all carry full sets).
- **AC2**: zero `dependencies.yaml` files exist under `workflows/`.
- **AC3**: zero `meta.yml` files carry a top-level `dependencies:` block.
- **AC4**: 44 `meta.yml` files yield 44 unique `artifact.id` values; substrate-neutral walk equals loader inventory.

Filed `.ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command: bash .ddx/executions/20260514T163049-8389e54b/verify_ac.sh`, which exits 0 (confirmed). No commit, no source changes — bead description's stated scope is satisfied by prior commits. If completing the richer field superset (artifact.prefix, id_format, prompts.review, etc.) across all 44 artifacts is the operator's actual intent, that's worth a separate bead; flagged in the rationale.
